tools.name: display_table
tools.description: REQUIRED for showing structured tabular data in the UI: SQL/ETL results, research comparisons, model release lists, timelines, or any row/column data. Call with headers (column names) and rows (each row = array of string cells). Renders a styled HTML table. Do NOT use markdown pipe tables for that data—call display_table first, then optional short prose. Use render_chart for graphs. Optional caption and style.

caption.description: Optional caption title for the table.

rows.description: List of rows, each row is an array of cell values.

style.description: Optional CSS style string to apply to the table element.

tools.name: render_chart
tools.description: Show a chart in the UI as a styled image. Prefer this over pasting long QuickChart URLs in markdown. Either pass chart_config (object or JSON string) as a Chart.js-style config for QuickChart (same as the `c` query parameter), or pass chart_url with an https URL to a chart image (e.g. QuickChart or image-charts). Use after summarizing data that benefits from a visual (bar, line, pie, etc.).

chart_config.description: QuickChart chart definition (Chart.js config object). Example: { type: 'bar', data: { labels: ['A','B'], datasets: [{ label: 'x', data: [1,2] }] } }

chart_url.description: Full https URL to a chart image (optional if chart_config is set).

tools.name: display_web_app
tools.description: Show a saved HTML/JS mini-app from the apps/ folder in a fullscreen modal in the dashboard. Pass the app slug (folder name), e.g. demo-counter. Create or edit apps with create_web_app / update_web_app.

tools.name: brave_search
tools.description: Search the web using Brave Search API. Returns raw JSON response.

tools.name: exec_terminal
tools.description: Execute a terminal command on the server and return its output. Modified to attempt admin/elevated execution when needed.

tools.name: get_gemini_response
tools.description: Calls Gemini REST API with GEMINI_API_KEY (separate from the chat provider). Prefer answering or summarizing with the main chat model when possible; use this only when you need an extra Gemini-only pass (different key or side experiment).

prompt.description: The text prompt to send to the Gemini model.

tools.name: brave_image_search
tools.description: Search the web using Brave Search and return the first image URL found for the query.

query.description: Search query to find an image for.

tools.name: embed_image
tools.description: Embeds an image URL as a markdown image tag for direct display.

url.description: The direct URL to an image.

tools.name: get_current_datetime
tools.description: Returns the current server date and time in ISO 8601 format.

tools.name: oklahoma_time
tools.description: Returns the current date and time in Oklahoma City (Central Time, America/Chicago).

tools.name: cron_job_manager
tools.description: Legacy helper: creates a scheduled agent job (cron) plus a human-readable line in memory/cron_jobs.md. The command text is passed to the agent as context at run time; it is NOT executed as a shell command. Prefer agent_cron_add for full control (at/every/cron, timezone, one-shot delete).

job_name.description: A unique name for the cron job.

command.description: What the agent should do (instructions); not run as OS shell.

tools.name: agent_cron_add
tools.description: Schedule an autonomous agent turn (OpenClaw-style). Persists to runtime/cron/jobs.json. schedule_kind: "at" (one-shot ISO 8601 in at), "every" (every_ms interval in milliseconds, min 5000), or "cron" (5-field cron in cron + optional timezone IANA). message is the user prompt sent to the model when the job fires. Optional provider, model, temperature override defaults from agent config. delete_after_run: for at-jobs only, remove the job from the store after a successful run (default false; set true for one-shot auto-cleanup).

message.description: Prompt for the agent when the job runs (prefixed with [cron:name] server-side).

at.description: ISO 8601 time when schedule_kind is at.

every_ms.description: Interval in milliseconds when schedule_kind is every (min 5000).

timezone.description: IANA timezone for cron schedules, e.g. America/Chicago. Defaults to PHP default timezone.

delete_after_run.description: For at-jobs: remove from the store after a successful run (default false).

enabled.description: Whether the job is active (default true).

tools.name: agent_cron_list
tools.description: List persisted agent cron jobs (id, schedule, last runs, message preview).

tools.name: agent_cron_remove
tools.description: Remove a scheduled agent job by job_id (from agent_cron_list).

tools.name: agent_cron_run_now
tools.description: Immediately run one agent turn for a persisted cron job (by job_id), without waiting for the schedule. Logs outcome to the job history.

tools.name: agent_cron_set_enabled
tools.description: Enable or disable a cron job without deleting it.

enabled.description: true to enable, false to pause.

tools.name: generate_image
tools.description: Generate an image using the Gemini API based on a textual description. Reads Gemini API key and endpoint from memory file gemini_api.md.

description.description: Textual description of the image to generate.

tools.name: show_website
tools.description: Display an iframe for a given URL. Returns HTML snippet with iframe.

url.description: The URL to embed in the iframe.

width.description: CSS width for the iframe (e.g., '100%' or '800px').

tools.name: create_and_display_web_app
tools.description: Creates an iframe that displays a given HTML/JS web app using srcdoc.

html_content.description: The full HTML content (including <script>, <style>, etc.) of the web app to display.

tools.name: show_tool_call_history
tools.description: Display a dropdown panel showing the complete history of all tool calls executed in the current session, with collapsible sections for each call showing the tool name, arguments, and result.

tools.name: inject_tool_history_js
tools.description: Generate JavaScript code that injects the tool call history dropdown panel into the AI response modal.
